title: CrossModal Study
description: The CrossModal Study is a neuroimaging dataset investigating cross-modal attention and target discrimination using MEG. Participants performed a cued target-discrimination task involving auditory and visual modalities, with frequency-tagged visual stimuli (36 Hz) and amplitude-modulated auditory stimuli (40 Hz) during a 3-second preparation interval. The dataset includes two experimental blocks: unimodal target presentation and cross-modal target-distractor conditions, with eye-tracking data to monitor oculomotor behavior.
methods_description: MEG data were acquired during a cued target-discrimination task. Participants received auditory or visual cues indicating target modality, followed by 3 seconds of task-irrelevant stimulation (36 Hz frequency-tagged fixation cross and 40 Hz amplitude-modulated tone). Targets were either visual Gabor patches or auditory tones (three difficulty levels each, calibrated individually). Block 1 presented unimodal targets; Block 2 presented targets with cross-modal distractors. Eye-link data were recorded to monitor eye movements. Participants were healthy controls with normal or corrected-to-normal vision and no history of psychiatric or neurological illness.
